metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_234
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Narp, a secreted protein, accumulates at the surface of excitatory synapses on live labelled cultured neurons (Fig. 3). In immuno-EM, Narp1 labeling is predominant within the synaptic cleft (Fig. 6). Its localization to the postsynaptic specialization membrane is in agreement with the interaction of Narp with the extracellular domains of heterologously expressed GluA1/2/3 and its formation of a complex with GluA1 in the brain (Fig. 7, 8).. Experimental description: Methods: "Membrane preparations (P2) were prepared from 4- to 6-week-old rat hippocampus". "Cultures of postnatal rat spinal cord neurons (...) were prepared."

8/3/2017 Pim:
-Species of My-NARP construct not stated in paper. Data on rat materials is sufficient for annotation, so rat species is approved for annotation.
- Narp overexpression construct is of rat species (see info in ticket #312)
